payload.query: According to some presidential scholars the United States has one president but two presidencies. Briefly review and critique the arguments concerning the two presidencies thesis. Does the two presidencies thesis still apply in the twenty first century given changes in presidential policymaking? What potential avenues might future work explore on this subject?

articles.title: Trump Says He Has Framework for Greenland Deal as NATO Mulls Idea of U.S. Sovereignty Over Bases
articles.abstract: President Trump’s announcement appeared to draw the United States back from the possibility of military and economic conflict with American allies over Greenland.
articles.snippet: President Trump’s announcement appeared to draw the United States back from the possibility of military and economic conflict with American allies over Greenland.
articles.byline: By Lara Jakes, Jim Tankersley and Zolan Kanno-Youngs

articles.title: You Break It, You Own It? Not for Trump When It Comes to Iran.
articles.abstract: “We’ll see what happens with the people,” President Trump said as he appeared to be distancing himself from the longer-term consequences of the war in Iran.
articles.snippet: “We’ll see what happens with the people,” President Trump said as he appeared to be distancing himself from the longer-term consequences of the war in Iran.
keywords: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

articles.title: Under Trump, Immigrants Are More Fearful but Determined to Stay, Poll Finds
articles.abstract: A national survey found that half of all immigrants in the United States say they feel less safe since President Trump took office. Still, many say their future remains bright.
articles.snippet: A national survey found that half of all immigrants in the United States say they feel less safe since President Trump took office. Still, many say their future remains bright.
articles.byline: By Luis Ferré-Sadurní, Ruth Igielnik, Ana Ley and Ashley Cai

articles.title: Trump Is Pushing the U.S.-Europe Alliance Onto a Precipice
articles.abstract: As President Trump tries to coerce European leaders over Greenland, they are pondering the unthinkable: Is an 80-year-old alliance doomed?
articles.snippet: As President Trump tries to coerce European leaders over Greenland, they are pondering the unthinkable: Is an 80-year-old alliance doomed?

articles.title: Trump Administration Says Europe Faces ‘Civilizational Erasure’
articles.abstract: America’s goal should be “to help Europe correct its current trajectory,” the administration said in its new National Security Strategy.
articles.snippet: America’s goal should be “to help Europe correct its current trajectory,” the administration said in its new National Security Strategy.
articles.byline: By Michael D. Shear, Jeanna Smialek and Lara Jakes

articles.title: Reviving Venezuela’s Flow of Oil Will Not Come Easily or Cheaply
articles.abstract: It will take years and billions in investment to revitalize Venezuela’s oil industry, and energy producers will likely be cautious before stepping in.
articles.snippet: It will take years and billions in investment to revitalize Venezuela’s oil industry, and energy producers will likely be cautious before stepping in.

articles.title: Zelensky Opens Way to Demilitarized Zone in Eastern Ukraine to Reach Peace
articles.abstract: The offer was the closest Mr. Zelensky has come to addressing the thorny territorial disputes in Donetsk that have repeatedly derailed peace talks.
articles.snippet: The offer was the closest Mr. Zelensky has come to addressing the thorny territorial disputes in Donetsk that have repeatedly derailed peace talks.

articles.title: Trump’s Tariffs Are Making Money. That May Make Them Hard to Quit.
articles.abstract: The tariffs are a substantial new source of revenue for the federal government. The budget may start to depend on it.
articles.snippet: The tariffs are a substantial new source of revenue for the federal government. The budget may start to depend on it.

articles.title: ‘We’re Not Stupid’: What Greenlanders Would Say to Trump
articles.abstract: A visit to Greenland reveals a swirl of feelings as people nervously await talks with the Trump administration about the island’s future.
articles.snippet: A visit to Greenland reveals a swirl of feelings as people nervously await talks with the Trump administration about the island’s future.
articles.byline: By Jeffrey Gettleman and Maya Tekeli

articles.title: Iranians Viewed by U.S. as Potential Leaders Died in Strikes, Trump Says
articles.abstract: With the future of Iran deeply uncertain, President Trump acknowledged that it could end up with a ruler “as bad” as Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
articles.snippet: With the future of Iran deeply uncertain, President Trump acknowledged that it could end up with a ruler “as bad” as Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

keywords: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
